title: psorospermum torrei
intro: Psorospermum torrei, also known as the yellow oleander, is a plant species native to Madagascar and other parts of the world, characterized by its toxic properties due to the presence of compounds such as oleandrin and neriine. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimalarial effects, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Psorospermum torrei include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of certain parasitic infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by boiling its leaves or stems in water, which can then be consumed as a tea or used as a topical treatment for various skin conditions.
